Bus Timings

RTA Dubai Feeder Bus Routes: How They Connect Your Journey

November 19, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Dubai’s expansive public transport network is designed to ensure seamless connectivity across the city, and the feeder bus routes play a crucial role in this system. Operated by the Roads and Transport Authority (RTA), these buses bridge the gap between metro stations and various neighborhoods, making commuting convenient and efficient.

Feeder Bus Routes

Feeder buses link key metro stations to various parts of Dubai, operating during metro hours. These routes facilitate smooth transitions between metro and bus travel, making it easier to reach your final destination.

Bus ID From To
F01 Union MS Al Baraha
F02 Etisalat MS Al Muhaisnah 4
F03 Al Rashidiya Bus Station Mirdiff West
F04 GGICO MS Sabkha, Turnoff
F05 Al Rashidiya Bus Station Mizhar 1
F06 Al Rashidiya Bus Station Al Rashidiya
F07 Etisalat MS Al Qusais DM Housing
F08 Dubai Festival City Al Nahda 2
F09 Al Jafiliya Bus Station (Dubai Frame) Al Wasl Park
F10 Al Rashidiya Bus Station Dubai Safari Park
F11 Financial Center MS Al Satwa
F12 MAX MS Al Wasl Park
F13 Dubai Mall MS The Dubai Mall
F14 Business Bay 2 Bus Station Business Bay MS
F15 Al Safa MS Al Qouz Industrial Area 2
F16 Al Safa MS Al Qouz, New Housing
F17 Al Safa MS Al Khail Gate
F18 Oud Metha MS Lamcy Plaza
F19A Bay Square Business Bay 2 Bus Station
F19B Business Bay 2 Bus Station Bay Square
F20 Business Bay MS Al Safa 1
F21 Al Nahda MS Al Nahda 2
F22 Stadium MS Qusais Industrial Area 2
F23 Stadium MS Al Nahda Terminus
F23A Stadium MS Al Nahda 1, Terminus 2
F24 Stadium MS Qusais Industrial Area 3
F25 Umm Al Sheif MS Al Quoz Industrial Area 3 & 4
F26 Al Safa MS Umm Al Sheif MS
F27 Al Jafiliya Bus Station Al Satwa
F28 Emirates Tower MS Satwa
F29 EQUITI MS Al Wasl Road
F30 Mall of Emirates Bus Station Dubai Studio City
F31 Dubai Internet City MS The Meadows East
F32 Mall of Emirates Bus Station The Sustainable City
F33 Mall of Emirates Bus Station Al Barsha 3
F34 DIC MS Dubai Production City
F35 Mall of Emirates Bus Station The Greens
F36 Mall of Emirates Bus Station Arjan
F37 Mall of Emirates Bus Station Sports City
F38 Jumeirah Golf Estates MS Dubai Sports City
F41 Business Bay MS Coral Tower
F43 Ibn Battuta Bus Station Discovery Gardens
F44 Energy MS Jebel Ali Gardens
F45 Al Furjan Discovery Gardens
F46 Ibn Battuta Bus Station DIP 2
F47 Danube MS DIP 2
F48 Danube MS DIP 2
F49 Danube MS Jebel Ali Industrial Area
F50 DIP MS DIP 2
F51 DIP MS DIP 1
F53 Danube MS Dubai Industrial City
F54 Danube MS JAFZA South
F55 Expo 2020 MS AMI Airport (PTB)
F56 DIC MS Al Khail MS
F57 Jebel Ali MS Bluewater Island
F60 Al Rashidiya Bus Station Downtown Mirdiff Gate
F61 Al Rashidiya Bus Station Al Warqa’a
F62 Nadd Al Hamar Emirates Metro Station
F70 Oud Metha Bus Station Dubai Museum
F81 Al Qusais Industrial Area Terminus 1 Al Nahda MS

Dubai Bus Fares

Dubai’s fare system is based on seven zones, with charges varying according to the distance traveled or number of zones crossed. Transfers between buses, metro, trams, and water buses within 30 minutes are charged as a single trip. Beyond this time frame, separate fares apply.

Fare Structure Using NOL Cards:

Fare according to Zone Silver Card Gold Card Blue Personal Card Red Card (Ticket)
Within 1 Zone AED 3 AED 6 AED 3 AED 4
2 Adjacent Zones AED 5 AED 10 AED 5 AED 6
More than 2 Zones AED 7.5 AED 15 AED 7.5 AED 8.5

Discounts are available for students, senior citizens, and residents with blue personalized cards. People of Determination and children under 5 travel for free. Fines are imposed for violations such as using invalid or expired cards, with a penalty of AED 200 or AED 500 for fake cards.

Facilities in RTA Dubai Buses

RTA buses and stations are equipped with various amenities:

  • Air-Conditioned Stations and Buses: Ensures comfort during travel.
Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE
  • Information Screens: Display stops and routes.
  • Accessibility Features: Includes spaces for People of Determination, alert sounds, and flashlight signals.
  • Additional Facilities: Vending machines, cafeterias, NOL top-up machines, dedicated sections for women and families, and bicycle parking.

General Guidelines on Using Public Buses in Dubai

  • Reserved Seating: Three rows behind the driver are for ladies and children.

  • Prohibitions: Eating, drinking, smoking, and creating noise are not allowed. Animals and heavy luggage are also prohibited.

Contact Information for RTA Dubai

For assistance, you can reach RTA Dubai through various channels:

  • Email: ASK@rta. ae

  • Phone: 800-90-90
  • Website: RTA Dubai Official Website
  • Customer Happiness Centres: Located in Deira, Al Aweer, Al Kifaf, Umm Ramool, Al Twar, Al Barsha, and Al Manarah.

Comparison of RTA Dubai Bus Route Types

Bus Route Type Purpose Coverage Area Key Characteristics
RTA Dubai Express Bus Routes Fast and direct connections to major destinations Major hubs and key locations within Dubai Fewer stops; faster travel time; prefixed with “E”
RTA Dubai Feeder Bus Routes Connects residential areas to main metro stations Residential neighborhoods and metro stations Smaller, local routes feeding into larger transport systems
RTA Urban CBD Bus Routes Serves the Central Business District (CBD) Central business areas of Dubai High frequency; caters to office commuters
RTA Urban Bus Routes General city transportation Various city areas across Dubai Extensive network; connects different neighborhoods and commercial areas
RTA Intercity Routes Links Dubai to neighboring cities Routes to cities like Abu Dhabi, Sharjah, Ajman, etc. Longer routes; prefixed with “E” for intercity travel

Feeder bus routes are essential for enhancing connectivity between metro stations and various neighborhoods in Dubai. By using these routes, commuters can efficiently transfer between different parts of the city, optimizing their travel experience. Understanding the feeder bus network will help you navigate Dubai’s public transport system with ease, ensuring a smooth and integrated journey across the Emirate.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: November 19, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
